939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`

945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text

958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it

962	    /// ```text

964	    /// ├── pypi
965	    /// │   ...
966	    /// │   ├── pandas

1	# Caching
2	
3	## Dependency caching
4	
5	uv uses aggressive caching to avoid re-downloading (and re-building) dependencies that have already
6	been accessed in prior runs.
7	
8	The specifics of uv's caching semantics vary based on the nature of the dependency:
9	
10	- **For registry dependencies** (like those downloaded from PyPI), uv respects HTTP caching headers.
11	- **For direct URL dependencies**, uv respects HTTP caching headers, and also caches based on the
12	  URL itself.
13	- **For Git dependencies**, uv caches based on the fully-resolved Git commit hash. As such,
14	  `uv pip compile` will pin Git dependencies to a specific commit hash when writing the resolved
15	  dependency set.
16	- **For local dependencies**, uv caches based on the last-modified time of the source archive (i.e.,
17	  the local `.whl` or `.tar.gz` file). For directories, uv caches based on the last-modified time of
18	  the `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, or `setup.cfg` file.
19	
20	If you're running into caching issues, uv includes a few escape hatches:
21	
22	- To clear the cache entirely, run `uv cache clean`. To clear the cache for a specific package, run
23	  `uv cache clean <package-name>`. For example, `uv cache clean ruff` will clear the cache for the
24	  `ruff` package.
25	- To force uv to revalidate cached data for all dependencies, pass `--refresh` to any command (e.g.,
26	  `uv sync --refresh` or `uv pip install --refresh ...`).
27	- To force uv to revalidate cached data for a specific dependency pass `--refresh-package` to any
28	  command (e.g., `uv sync --refresh-package ruff` or `uv pip install --refresh-package ruff ...`).
29	- To force uv to ignore existing installed versions, pass `--reinstall` to any installation command
30	  (e.g., `uv sync --reinstall` or `uv pip install --reinstall ...`). (Consider running
31	  `uv cache clean <package-name>` first, to ensure that the cache is cleared prior to
32	  reinstallation.)
33	
34	As a special case, uv will always rebuild and reinstall any local directory dependencies passed
35	explicitly on the command-line (e.g., `uv pip install .`).
36	
37	## Dynamic metadata
38	
39	By default, uv will _only_ rebuild and reinstall local directory dependencies (e.g., editables) if
40	the `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, or `setup.cfg` file in the directory root has changed, or if a
41	`src` directory is added or removed. This is a heuristic and, in some cases, may lead to fewer
42	re-installs than desired.
43	
44	To incorporate additional information into the cache key for a given package, you can add cache key
45	entries under [`tool.uv.cache-keys`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/reference/settings/#cache-keys),
46	which covers both file paths and Git commit hashes. Setting
47	[`tool.uv.cache-keys`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/reference/settings/#cache-keys) will replace
48	defaults, so any necessary files (like `pyproject.toml`) should still be included in the
49	user-defined cache keys.
50	
51	For example, if a project specifies dependencies in `pyproject.toml` but uses
52	[`setuptools-scm`](https://pypi.org/project/setuptools-scm/) to manage its version, and should thus
53	be rebuilt whenever the commit hash or dependencies change, you can add the following to the
54	project's `pyproject.toml`:
55	
56	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
57	[tool.uv]
58	cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, { git = { commit = true } }]
59	```
60	
61	If your dynamic metadata incorporates information from the set of Git tags, you can expand the cache
62	key to include the tags:
63	
64	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
65	[tool.uv]
66	cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, { git = { commit = true, tags = true } }]
67	```
68	
69	Similarly, if a project reads from a `requirements.txt` to populate its dependencies, you can add
70	the following to the project's `pyproject.toml`:
71	
72	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
73	[tool.uv]
74	cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, { file = "requirements.txt" }]
75	```
76	
77	Globs are supported for `file` keys, following the syntax of the
78	[`glob`](https://docs.rs/glob/0.3.1/glob/struct.Pattern.html) crate. For example, to invalidate the
79	cache whenever a `.toml` file in the project directory or any of its subdirectories is modified, use
80	the following:
81	
82	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
83	[tool.uv]
84	cache-keys = [{ file = "**/*.toml" }]
85	```
86	
87	!!! note
88	
89	    The use of globs can be expensive, as uv may need to walk the filesystem to determine whether any files have changed.
90	    This may, in turn, requiring traversal of large or deeply nested directories.
91	
92	Similarly, if a project relies on an environment variable, you can add the following to the
93	project's `pyproject.toml` to invalidate the cache whenever the environment variable changes:
94	
95	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
96	[tool.uv]
97	cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, { env = "MY_ENV_VAR" }]
98	```
99	
100	Finally, to invalidate a project whenever a specific directory (like `src`) is created or removed,
101	add the following to the project's `pyproject.toml`:
102	
103	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
104	[tool.uv]
105	cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, { dir = "src" }]
106	```
107	
108	Note that the `dir` key will only track changes to the directory itself, and not arbitrary changes
109	within the directory.
110	
111	As an escape hatch, if a project uses `dynamic` metadata that isn't covered by `tool.uv.cache-keys`,
112	you can instruct uv to _always_ rebuild and reinstall it by adding the project to the
113	`tool.uv.reinstall-package` list:
114	
115	```toml title="pyproject.toml"
116	[tool.uv]
117	reinstall-package = ["my-package"]
118	```
119	
120	This will force uv to rebuild and reinstall `my-package` on every run, regardless of whether the
121	package's `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, or `setup.cfg` file has changed.
122	
123	## Cache safety
124	
125	It's safe to run multiple uv commands concurrently, even against the same virtual environment. uv's
126	cache is designed to be thread-safe and append-only, and thus robust to multiple concurrent readers
127	and writers. uv applies a file-based lock to the target virtual environment when installing, to
128	avoid concurrent modifications across processes.
129	
130	Note that it's _never_ safe to modify the cache directly (e.g., by removing a file or directory).
131	
132	## Clearing the cache
133	
134	uv provides a few different mechanisms for removing entries from the cache:
135	
136	- `uv cache clean` removes _all_ cache entries from the cache directory, clearing it out entirely.
137	- `uv cache clean ruff` removes all cache entries for the `ruff` package, useful for invalidating
138	  the cache for a single or finite set of packages.
139	- `uv cache prune` removes all _unused_ cache entries. For example, the cache directory may contain
140	  entries created in previous uv versions that are no longer necessary and can be safely removed.
141	  `uv cache prune` is safe to run periodically, to keep the cache directory clean.
142	
143	uv blocks cache-modifying operations while other uv commands are running. By default, those
144	`uv cache` commands have a 5 min timeout waiting for other uv processes to terminate to avoid
145	deadlocks. This timeout can be changed with
146	[`UV_LOCK_TIMEOUT`](../reference/environment.md#uv_lock_timeout). In cases where it is known that no
147	other uv processes are reading or writing from the cache, `--force` can be used to ignore the lock.
148	
149	## Caching in continuous integration
150	
151	It's common to cache package installation artifacts in continuous integration environments (like
152	GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) to speed up subsequent runs.
153	
154	By default, uv caches both the wheels that it builds from source and the pre-built wheels that it
155	downloads directly, to enable high-performance package installation.
156	
157	However, in continuous integration environments, persisting pre-built wheels may be undesirable.
158	With uv, it turns out that it's often faster to _omit_ pre-built wheels from the cache (and instead
159	re-download them from the registry on each run). On the other hand, caching wheels that are built
160	from source tends to be worthwhile, since the wheel building process can be expensive, especially
161	for extension modules.
162	
163	To support this caching strategy, uv provides a `uv cache prune --ci` command, which removes all
164	pre-built wheels and unzipped source distributions from the cache, but retains any wheels that were
165	built from source. We recommend running `uv cache prune --ci` at the end of your continuous
166	integration job to ensure maximum cache efficiency. For an example, see the
167	[GitHub integration guide](../guides/integration/github.md#caching).
168	
169	## Cache directory
170	
171	uv determines the cache directory according to, in order:
172	
173	1. A temporary cache directory, if `--no-cache` was requested.
174	2. The specific cache directory specified via `--cache-dir`, `UV_CACHE_DIR`, or
175	   [`tool.uv.cache-dir`](../reference/settings.md#cache-dir).
176	3. A system-appropriate cache directory, e.g., `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/uv` or `$HOME/.cache/uv` on Unix and
177	   `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows
178	
179	!!! note
180	
181	    uv _always_ requires a cache directory. When `--no-cache` is requested, uv will still use
182	    a temporary cache for sharing data within that single invocation.
183	
184	    In most cases, `--refresh` should be used instead of `--no-cache` — as it will update the cache
185	    for subsequent operations but not read from the cache.
186	
187	It is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same file system as the
188	Python environment uv is operating on. Otherwise, uv will not be able to link files from the cache
189	into the environment and will instead need to fallback to slow copy operations.
190	
191	## Cache versioning
192	
193	The uv cache is composed of a number of buckets (e.g., a bucket for wheels, a bucket for source
194	distributions, a bucket for Git repositories, and so on). Each bucket is versioned, such that if a
195	release contains a breaking change to the cache format, uv will not attempt to read from or write to
196	an incompatible cache bucket.
197	
198	For example, uv 0.4.13 included a breaking change to the core metadata bucket. As such, the bucket
199	version was increased from v12 to v13. Within a cache version, changes are guaranteed to be both
200	forwards- and backwards-compatible.

```rust
pub(crate) fn cache_size(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    // 1. Warn about experimental feature
    if !preview.is_enabled(PreviewFeature::CacheSize) {
        warn_user!(...);
    }

    // 2. Handle empty cache
    if !cache.root().exists() {
        if human_readable {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0B")?;
        } else {
            writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "0")?;
        }
        return Ok(ExitStatus::Success);
    }

    // 3. Compute total disk usage using diskus crate
    let disk_usage = DiskUsage::new(vec![cache.root().to_path_buf()]);
    let total_bytes = disk_usage.count_ignoring_errors();

    // 4. Format and output
    if human_readable {
        let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
    } else {
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
    }

    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

**Key details:**

- The `count_ignoring_errors()` method walks the entire cache tree
- Uses `human_readable_bytes()` utility from `uv_console` to format output

931	/// The different kinds of data in the cache are stored in different bucket, which in our case
932	/// are subdirectories of the cache root.
933	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]
934	pub enum CacheBucket {
935	    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), alongside their metadata and cache policy.
936	    ///
937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text
954	    /// # pypi wheel
955	    /// pandas
956	    /// # url wheel
957	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961	    /// doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962	    /// ```text
963	    /// wheel-v0
964	    /// ├── pypi
965	    /// │   ...
966	    /// │   ├── pandas
967	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
968	    /// │   ...
969	    /// └── url
970	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
971	    ///         └── flask
972	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
973	    /// ```
974	    ///
975	    /// We get the following `requirement.txt` from `pip compile`:
976	    ///
977	    /// ```text
978	    /// [...]
979	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum PreviewFeature {
    PythonInstallDefault = 1 << 0,
    PythonUpgrade = 1 << 1,
    JsonOutput = 1 << 2,
    Pylock = 1 << 3,
    AddBounds = 1 << 4,
    PackageConflicts = 1 << 5,
    ExtraBuildDependencies = 1 << 6,
    DetectModuleConflicts = 1 << 7,
    Format = 1 << 8,
    NativeAuth = 1 << 9,
    S3Endpoint = 1 << 10,
    CacheSize = 1 << 11,
    InitProjectFlag = 1 << 12,
    WorkspaceMetadata = 1 << 13,
    WorkspaceDir = 1 << 14,
    WorkspaceList = 1 << 15,
    SbomExport = 1 << 16,
    AuthHelper = 1 << 17,
    DirectPublish = 1 << 18,
    TargetWorkspaceDiscovery = 1 << 19,
    MetadataJson = 1 << 20,
    GcsEndpoint = 1 << 21,
    AdjustUlimit = 1 << 22,
    SpecialCondaEnvNames = 1 << 23,
    RelocatableEnvsDefault = 1 << 24,
    PublishRequireNormalized = 1 << 25,
    Audit = 1 << 26,
    ProjectDirectoryMustExist = 1 << 27,
}

impl PreviewFeature {
    /// Returns the string representation of a single preview feature flag.
    fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::PythonInstallDefault => "python-install-default",
            Self::PythonUpgrade => "python-upgrade",
            Self::JsonOutput => "json-output",
            Self::Pylock => "pylock",
            Self::AddBounds => "add-bounds",
            Self::PackageConflicts => "package-conflicts",
            Self::ExtraBuildDependencies => "extra-build-dependencies",
            Self::DetectModuleConflicts => "detect-module-conflicts",
            Self::Format => "format",
            Self::NativeAuth => "native-auth",
            Self::S3Endpoint => "s3-endpoint",
            Self::CacheSize => "cache-size",
            Self::InitProjectFlag => "init-project-flag",
            Self::WorkspaceMetadata => "workspace-metadata",
            Self::WorkspaceDir => "workspace-dir",
            Self::WorkspaceList => "workspace-list",
            Self::SbomExport => "sbom-export",
            Self::AuthHelper => "auth-helper",
            Self::DirectPublish => "direct-publish",
            Self::TargetWorkspaceDiscovery => "target-workspace-discovery",
            Self::MetadataJson => "metadata-json",
            Self::GcsEndpoint => "gcs-endpoint",
            Self::AdjustUlimit => "adjust-ulimit",
            Self::SpecialCondaEnvNames => "special-conda-env-names",
            Self::RelocatableEnvsDefault => "relocatable-envs-default",
            Self::PublishRequireNormalized => "publish-require-normalized",
            Self::Audit => "audit",
            Self::ProjectDirectoryMustExist => "project-directory-must-exist",
        }
    }
}

impl Display for PreviewFeature {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}", self.as_str())
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Error, Clone)]
#[error("Unknown feature flag")]
pub struct PreviewFeatureParseError;

impl FromStr for PreviewFeature {
    type Err = PreviewFeatureParseError;

    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
        Ok(match s {
            "python-install-default" => Self::PythonInstallDefault,
            "python-upgrade" => Self::PythonUpgrade,
            "json-output" => Self::JsonOutput,
            "pylock" => Self::Pylock,
            "add-bounds" => Self::AddBounds,
            "package-conflicts" => Self::PackageConflicts,
            "extra-build-dependencies" => Self::ExtraBuildDependencies,
            "detect-module-conflicts" => Self::DetectModuleConflicts,
            "format" => Self::Format,
            "native-auth" => Self::NativeAuth,
            "s3-endpoint" => Self::S3Endpoint,
            "gcs-endpoint" => Self::GcsEndpoint,
            "cache-size" => Self::CacheSize,
            "init-project-flag" => Self::InitProjectFlag,
            "workspace-metadata" => Self::WorkspaceMetadata,
            "workspace-dir" => Self::WorkspaceDir,
            "workspace-list" => Self::WorkspaceList,
            "sbom-export" => Self::SbomExport,
            "auth-helper" => Self::AuthHelper,
            "direct-publish" => Self::DirectPublish,
            "target-workspace-discovery" => Self::TargetWorkspaceDiscovery,
            "metadata-json" => Self::MetadataJson,
            "adjust-ulimit" => Self::AdjustUlimit,
            "special-conda-env-names" => Self::SpecialCondaEnvNames,
            "relocatable-envs-default" => Self::RelocatableEnvsDefault,
            "publish-require-normalized" => Self::PublishRequireNormalized,
            "audit" => Self::Audit,
            "project-directory-must-exist" => Self::ProjectDirectoryMustExist,
            _ => return Err(PreviewFeatureParseError),
        })

use assert_cmd::assert::OutputAssertExt;

use uv_test::uv_snapshot;

/// Test that `cache stats` shows all buckets as 0 for an empty cache.
#[test]
fn cache_stats_empty() {
    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
